# {{ logo }} Namer

{{ _ci_badge }} {{ _codecov_badge }} {{ _pypi_badge }} {{ _pypi_download_badge }} {{ _download_badge }} {{ _download_month_badge }}

Namer is a powerful web app, folder watchdog and command line tool for renaming video files and tagging mp4 video files in a way that helps plex/jellyfin/emby and related plugins extract that data or lookup data with the [PornDB]'s plugins for plex or jellyfin/emby.

Namer is easily installed as a python pip and can:

- namer now support perceptual hashes by default and can identify video files that don't have parsable names.

- can be run watching a directory for new files to name, tag and move to an output location, possible setting file permissions, writing .nfo files with downloaded images, attempting to grab trailers, and retrying failed files nightly.

  `python -m namer watchdog`

- while running watchdog, will also have a webui that can be used to manually match and rename any files that could not be automatically matched. You can set the webroot, port, bound ip, enable/disable in your namer.cfg file.

  `http://<ip>:6980/`

- can be used to name and embed tags in individual files with metadata from porndb:

  `python -m namer rename -f /path/to/file/Site.[YY]YY.MM.DD.MessOfText.XXX.2160.mp4 [-v]`

- can be used to name and tag files with metadata from a jellyfin/emby/kodi .nfo file (should be named the same as the file except for extension).

  `python -m namer rename -f /path/to/file/Site.[YY]YY.MM.DD.MessOfText.XXX.2160.mp4 -i [-v]`

- can be used to rename a tag a file based on the directory name, so if you have a file like `/Site.[YY]YY.MM.DD.MessOfText.XXX.2160/abc.mp4`

  `python -m namer rename -d /path/to/dir/Site.[YY]YY.MM.DD.MessOfText.XXX.2160/`

- can be used to rename a tag a whole mess of dirs and files in a directory (using -m, meaning "many").

  `python -m namer rename -m -d /path/to/dir/`

- can be used to just suggest a possible name. The file doesn't need to exist but should have an extension.

  `python -m namer suggest -f Site.[YY]YY.MM.DD.MessOfText.XXX.2160.mp4`

For all of the above it's recommended to have a config file in your home directory (copied from namer/namer.cfg.default in this git repo)

Also provided is a docker file if you prefer.

It is possible to ignore and not need to parse dates for studios added to a list in the configuration file. This is mostly used for studios that do not list dates on videos.

## Why should I use this?

1. You have partially well structured file names (say from an rss feed, etc) and you never want to have to manually match files in plex/jellyfin/emby with the [PornDB]'s plugin.
2. You don't want your recent videos to be added to your library until they are matchable in the [PornDB].
3. You want to store the metadata about a file in mp4 files, in a way that can be read by Apple TV app, including information like: Studio, date created, name, performers, original url, proper HD tags, ratings, and movie poster. All of this data is readable by Plex, and most by Jellyfin/Emby in case you want to standard Apple video players or your library's metadata storage is ever damaged.

## How successful at matching videos is this tool?

For data pulled from the internet with rss feeds (which are often in the file format listed below) .... very near perfect. The author and others have only experienced two mismatches, and those type of failures can never occur again.

If running in a background watchdog mode, files that were failed to match are retried every 24 hours, letting the [PornDB] scrapers catch up with any metadata they may be missing.

Optionally, a log file can be enabled to show the original file name parts, what options were evaluated, and which match was used to name the file, it will be written next to your video file with the same name as the file (with a \_namer.log) suffix rather than an mp4/mkv/avi/mov/flv extension. This is very useful for sanity checking matches, and if ever a mismatch does occur the original file name is available in the log.

## For the curious, how is a match made?

Namer assumes that file names exist as in a format like `` `sitename-[YY]YY-MM-DD-Scene.and.or.performer.name.mp4.` ``. A powerful regex tries to determine the various parts of a file's name. Note that the separating dashes and dots above are interchangeable, and spaces may also be used as separators (or any number of any combo of the three.) This regex is overridable, but you really need to know what you're doing and if you don't have all the match groups for the regex, the match from the the [PornDB] will likely not be any where near as robust as it is with a site, a date, and a scene/perform name section.
You'll have to read the code to figure out how to set this. You really shouldn't do it.

When determining a possible queried match from the [PornDB]:

Sitename my not need be the full name of the site, as long as a the looked up sitename starts with file's sitename it could be a valid match.

The date may have a four digit or two digit year. If two digit, "20" is assumed as the default century, not "19". A potential match must be with one day plus/minus the file's date to be considered a match.

Finally the looked up scene name and all performers first and last names are combined in to what is called a powerset (every combo of including or not including each artist and/or scene name), and that is compared against the file's 'Scene.and.or.performer.name' section with a tool called rapidfuzz. A name must be 95% similar to a member of the powerset to be considered a match, though all potential matches are evaluated and sorted before selecting the best match. Information about all potential matches are stored in the local log file if it is enabled.

At the same time name based matching is occurring, a perceptual hash is built and queried against the [PornDB].

All matches, regardless of source are made unique with the [PornDB]'s UUID (universally unique identifier). If more than one unique match exist, or no match exists but partial name matches exist, all data is available in the web ui for users to select the correct match.

## I'm sold how do I install it!

You have two choices. Do you use docker? Pull the docker image, here's docker-compose snippet to help you out.

```yaml
version: "3"
services:
  namer:
    container_name: namer
    image: ghcr.io/theporndatabase/namer:latest
    environment:
      - PUID=1001
      - PGID=1000
      - TZ=America/Los_Angeles
      - NAMER_CONFIG=/config/namer.cfg
    volumes:
      - /apps/namer/:/config # <- this will store the namer.cfg file copied by you from the git repo ( namer/namer.cfg.default )
      - /media:/data # <- this will have the four folders namer needs to work, referenced in the namer.cfg file you create.
  healthcheck: # <- if on a qnap nas, the default health check will not work for you, domain name is the container_name
    test: [ "CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://namer:6980/<replace with your webroot>/api/healthcheck || exit 1" ]
    interval: 1m
    timeout: 30s
    # retries: 3
    # start_period: 40s
  restart: always
```

Copy namer.cfg to your config location (a path mapped to /config/namer.cfg above), and set values for your setup.
The config is well commented and you should only need to add a token for the porndb and change file locations.

Running a service will occur automatically once you call `docker-compose up`. Now check out the configuration section below.

## Pip/Python usage

A detailed install tutorial can be found at [Install Instructions]

______________________________________________________________________

There is a well documented template of namer.cfg in this git repo, which is broken up in to three sections.
One section is related to command line renaming, the [namer section], one related to tagging mp4s [metadata section],
and finally one related to the watchdog process [watchdog section].
Please note that the [namer section] section and the [watchdog section]
section both have a field to describe the new name of a file based on looked up metadata from the [PornDB].
They differ because when run from the command line namer will keep the file "in place".
If namer is passed a dir on the command line as input it can operate in one of two modes,
the default mode is to look for the largest mp4 file, or other configured movie file extension if no mp4 exists,
and rename and move that file to the root of the folder (if it's in a sub-folder).
In this case, by default the assumption is the name of the folder should be parsed to look for information to
search the [PornDB] for matching rather than the file name. Meaning,
if you pass a file to namer on the commandline it will be renamed but stay in the same directory.

## Typical Watchdog Behavior:

The watchdog process will watch a single folder, configured with [watch_dir] in the `namer.cfg` file. Any new files and directories that appear in the [watch_dir]
will be processed once an mp4/mkv/avi/mov/flv file has been fully copied in to it.

The first step in processing is to more the newly appearing directory or file in to the [work_dir].

Once moved the processing is highly dependant on the namer.cfg file, but in general, the name of video file or the directory file (configured with `prefer_dir_name_if_available` flag)
is parsed and matched with a scene from the [PornDB]. See [For the curious, how is a match made?]. If a match cannot be made the general assumption is that the [PornDB] doesn't have metadata for that file yet.
The file is move to the failed dir [fail_dir] to be retried once a day at a time configured with [retry_time],
which by default will be a random selected minute in the 3am hour of your timezone. If [enabled_tagging] flag is set to true then
the metadata (including cover art if [enable_poster] is set) will be embedded in the mp4 file. Please read the comments in the namer.cfg to find out about genres, tags, performers, etc.

Finally, the file is moved to a location defined by [dest_dir] and [new_relative_path_name].

## Development

```sh
# Install Python
# Install poetry
# Install pnpm
# Install golang

# Install poe the poet
poetry self add 'poethepoet[poetry_plugin]'
# or
pip add poethepoet

# Set python version if not using the latest python as your default
poetry env use 3.11

# Build everything
poetry run poe build_all

# Code Coverage:
poetry run pytest --cov

# Html Coverage report:
poetry run coverage html

# Local python install
pip install ./dist/namer-<version>.tar.gz

# Publishing:
# First make sure you have set gotten a token from pypi and set it on your machine.
poetry config pypi-token.pypi <token>

# Perhaps update the version number?
poetry version <new number>

# Publishing a release to pypi.org:
poetry publish

# build docker file with:
./docker_build.sh
```

## Pull Requests Are Welcome!

Just be sure to pay attention to the tests and any failing pylint results. If you want to vet a pr will be accepted before building code, file an new feature request issue, and 4c0d3r will comment on it and set you up for success. Tests are must.

classes.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.config_file
documentation: Location of config file used to generate this config.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.config_updater
documentation: Configuration for namer and namer_watchdog

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.porndb_token
documentation:
token to access porndb.
sign up here: https://theporndb.net/

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.name_parser
documentation:
This config may be a regex you provide, or a set of token used to build a regex.

By default names are of the form:
`Site.YYYY.MM.DD.TS.scene.name.mkv`,\`\`Site.YYYY.MM.DD.scene.name.mkv\`\`,\`\`Site.TS.scene.name.mkv\`\`,\`\`Site.scene.name.mkv\`\`

> Supported token:

`` `
_sep            r'[\.\- ]+'
_site           r'(?P<site>[a-zA-Z0-9\.\-\ ]*?[a-zA-Z0-9]*?)'
_date           r'(?P<year>[0-9]{2}(?:[0-9]{2})?)[\.\- ]+(?P<month>[0-9]{2})[\.\- ]+(?P<day>[0-9]{2})'
_optional_date  r'(?:(?P<year>[0-9]{2}(?:[0-9]{2})?)[\.\- ]+(?P<month>[0-9]{2})[\.\- ]+(?P<day>[0-9]{2})[\.\- ]+)?'
_ts             r'((?P<trans>[T|t][S|s])'+_sep+'){0,1}'
_name           r'(?P<name>(?:.(?![0-9]{2,4}[\.\- ][0-9]{2}[\.\- ][0-9]{2}))*)'
_dot            r'\.'
_ext            r'(?P<ext>[a-zA-Z0-9]{3,4})$'
` ``

The parts extracted from the file will be used in matching.
\_ts is optional, and is mostly useful in stripping the marker from the \_name, aiding in matching.
\_date if present is used to ensure the match is within 24 hours of your date.
If the year/month/day capture groups are not present (due to you not using the supplied \_date regex)
dates will not be used in matching, possibly allowing false positives.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.inplace_name
documentation:
How to write output file name.  When namer.py is run this is applied in place
(next to the file to be processed).
When run from namer_watchdog.py this will be written in to the success dir.

Supports stand python 3 formatting, as well as:
* a prefix:
{site: 1p} - in this case put one space in front of the 'site', so ' Vixen'
* a suffix:
{date:_1s} - in this case put one underscore after the 'date', so '2020-01-01_'
* an infix:
{date:_2i} - in this case put two underscore before and after the 'date', so '__2020-01-01__'

Examples:
* {site} - {date} - {scene}.{ext}
* {site}/{date}.{scene}.{ext}

Missing values will be ignored.

Allowed replacements:

* 'date' - in the format of YYYY-MM-DD.
* 'description' - too long, don't use in a name.
* 'name' - the scene name
* 'site' - the site name, BrazzersExxtra, AllHerLuv, Deeper, etc with spaces removed.
* 'full_site' - the site name from porndb, unmodified, i.e: Brazzers Exxtra, All Her Luv, etc.
* 'parent' - the parent site/studio name from porndb, unmodified, i.e: Brazzers, etc.
* 'full_parent' - the parent full site/studio name from porndb, unmodified, i.e: Brazzers, Vixen, etc.
* 'network' - the network site or studio name, MindGeek, Vixen, etc with spaces removed.
* 'full_network' - the network full site/studio name from porndb, unmodified, i.e: Mind Geek, Vixen, etc.
* 'performers' - comma seperated list of female performers
* 'all_performers' - comma seperated list of all performers
* 'act' - an act, parsed from original file name, don't use.
* 'ext' - original file's extension, you should keep this.
* 'trans' - 'TS', or 'ts' if detected in original file name.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.min_file_size
documentation: minimum file size to process in MB, ignored if a file is to be processed

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.convert_container_to
documentation:
Without modify the enconding where possible covert container (aka file types) to this
desired container type ("mp4", "mkv", "avi", ect), plugged in to the command:
"ffmpeg -i input.mkv -c copy output.<type>"

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.preserve_duplicates
documentation:
should duplicate videos be preserved.
If false, duplicates will be verified with a perceptual hash, then compared via ffprobe.
* First any movie under two minutes is ignored and presumed to be a sample,
* then the highest resolution video is selected
* then the best encoding mechanism h.245, or hvec.
You can set min file size to 0 if you have set

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.max_desired_resolutions
documentation:
Videos above this resolution will not be retained if the exact match to your resolution (or less) is available.
List your desired resolution: 4380 2160 1080 or 720, -1 indicates that no max is desired.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.desired_codec
documentation: Listed in order, desired codecs defaults to "hvec h246", most videos are still in h246 encoding.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.prefer_dir_name_if_available
documentation: If a directory name is to be preferred over a file name.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.target_extensions
documentation: File types namer targets, only 'mp4's can be tagged, others can be renamed.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.write_namer_log
documentation: Should a log of comparisons be written next to processed video files.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.write_namer_failed_log
documentation: Should a log of comparisons be written next to processed video files.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.update_permissions_ownership
documentation:
Should file permissions/ownership be updated.
If false, set_uid/set_gid,set_dir_permissions,set_file_permissions will be ignored

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.set_uid
documentation: UID Settings for new/moved files/dirs.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.set_gid
documentation: GID Settings for new/moved files/dirs.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.set_dir_permissions
documentation: Permissions Settings for new/moved dirs.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.set_file_permissions
documentation: Permissions Settings for new/moved file.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.max_performer_names
documentation:
When guessing at matches namer can use performer names to attempt to match.   This can be costly in terms of cpu time.
Plus who lists all the performers if over 6 on a scene's name?   You can increase this, but the cpu/runtime cost increases
rapidly (combinatorial)

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.write_nfo
documentation: Write an nfo file next to the directory in an emby/jellyfin readable format.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.trailer_location
namer.configuration.NamerConfig.sites_with_no_date_info
documentation:
A list of site names that do not have proper date information in them.   This is a problem with some tpdb
scrapers/storage mechanisms.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.site_abbreviations
documentation:
Configuration provided list of abbreviations, should the site of a parsed file name match the abbreviation (key),
it will be replaced with the value matching the key, like ["aa","Amature Allure"].   It is up to the user to provide
a list of abbreviations.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.movie_data_preferred
documentation: Sequence of sites where movie data should be prefered (movies will be marked scenes instead of movie)

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.vr_studios
documentation: Sequence of vr studios who's content is all vr content.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.vr_tags
documentation: a set of tags that indicates an individual video is vr.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.database_path
documentation: Path where stores namer system data.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.use_database
documentation: Use namer database.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.use_requests_cache
documentation: Cache http requests

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.requests_cache_expire_minutes
documentation: Amount of minutes that http request would be in cache

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.plex_hack
documentation: Should plex movies have S##E## stripped out of movie names (to allow videos to be visible in plex)

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.path_cleanup
documentation: Cleanup final filename

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.override_tpdb_address
documentation:
Used only for testing, can override the location of the porn database - usually to point at a locally
running server that responds like tpdb to predefined queries.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.enabled_tagging
documentation:
Currently metadata pulled from the porndb can be added to mp4 files.
This metadata will be read in fully by Plex, and Apple TV app, partially by Jellyfin (no artist support).
Metadata includes, Title, Release Date, Scene Name, Artist, Source URL, XXX Movie rating.
If a file is an mkv adding metadata at this time isn't supported.

Should metadata fetched from the porn db be written in to the metadata of the mp4.
No flags in this section will be used if this is not set to true.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.enabled_poster
documentation:
Should the poster fetched from the porn db be written in to the metadata of the mp4.
This poster will be displayed in Plex, Jellyfin and Apple TV app.
Only applicable if enabled_tagging is True

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.download_type
documentation: List of which images would be downloaded

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.image_format
documentation: Downloaded images format: png, jpeg

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.enable_metadataapi_genres
documentation:
Should genres pulled from the porndb be added to the file?   These genres are noisy and
not recommend for use.  If this is false a single default genre will be used.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.default_genre
documentation:
If genre's are not copied this is the default genre added to files.
Default value is adult.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.language
documentation:
if language is set it will be used to select the default audio stream in an mp4 that has too many default stream
to play correct in quicktime/apple tv.   If the language isn't found or is already the only default no action is
taken, no streams (audio/video) are re-encoded.  Available here: https://iso639-3.sil.org/code_tables/639/data/

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.search_phash
documentation: Calculate and use phashes in search for matches

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.send_phash
documentation: If match was made via name, or user selection and not phash, send the phash (only functions if search_phash is true)

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.use_alt_phash_tool
documentation: Use alternative phash generator (might be faster, not 100% compatible)

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.max_ffmpeg_workers
documentation: Max ffmpeg processes for alternative phash generation, empty for auto select

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.use_gpu
documentation: Use gpu for alternative phash generation

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.mark_collected
documentation: Mark any matched video as "collected" in tpdb, allowing tpdb to keep track of videos you have collected.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.require_match_phash_top
documentation: If there is a phash match, require any name match be in the top N results

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.ignored_dir_regex
documentation: If a file found in the watch dir matches this regex it will be ignored, useful for some file processes.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.new_relative_path_name
documentation:
like inplace_name above used for local call for renaming, this instead is used to move a file/dir to a location relative
to the dest_dir below on successful matching/tagging.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.del_other_files
documentation:
when processing a directory in the dest dir, should extra files besides the largest video be removed?
or attempt to move them to a subdirectory specified in new_relative_path_name, if one exist.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.watch_dir
documentation: If running in watchdog mode, director where new downloads go.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.work_dir
documentation:
If running in watchdog mode, temporary directory where work is done.
a log file shows attempted matches and match closeness.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.failed_dir
documentation:
If running in watchdog mode, Should processing fail the file or directory is moved here.
Files can be manually moved to watch-dir to force reprocessing.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.dest_dir
documentation: If running in watchdog mode, dir where finalized files get written.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.retry_time
documentation: Time to retry failed items every day.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.extra_sleep_time
documentation: Extra time to sleep in seconds to allow all information to be copied in dir

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.queue_limit
documentation: Maximum amount of items in queue

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.queue_sleep_time
documentation: Sleep time between queue size check

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.web
documentation: Run webserver while running watchdog

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.port
documentation: Web server port

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.host
documentation: Web server host

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.web_root
documentation: webroot (root url to place pages), useful for reverse proxies

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.allow_delete_files
documentation: Allow to delete files in web interface

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.add_columns_from_log
documentation: Add columns from failed log to table in web interface

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.add_complete_column
documentation: Add creation date from failed log to table in web interface

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.cache_session
documentation: If use_requests_cache is true this http.session will be constructed and used for requests to tpdb.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.debug
documentation: Set logger level to debug

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.console_format
documentation: Set logger output format

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.manual_mode
documentation: If True, successful matches will go to failed directory

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.diagnose_errors
documentation:
Errors may be raised by the program, and when they are loguru may be used to help explain them, showing
values in the stack trace, potentially including the porndb token, this setting should only be turned on
if you are going to check an logs you share for your token.

class_variables.name: namer.configuration.NamerConfig.vph
documentation: type: ignore
noinspection PyDataclass

modules.name: namer.fileinfo
documentation: Parse string in to FileNamePart define in namer_types.

functions.name: namer.fileinfo.name_cleaner
documentation:
Given the name parts, following a date, but preceding the file extension, attempt to glean
extra information and discard useless information for matching with the porndb.

functions.name: namer.fileinfo.parse_file_name
documentation:
Given an input name of the form site-yy.mm.dd-some.name.part.1.XXX.2160p.mp4,
parses out the relevant information in to a structure form.

classes.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.site
documentation: Site the file originated from, "DorcelClub", "EvilAngel", etc.

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.date
documentation: formatted: YYYY-mm-dd

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.trans
documentation:
If the name originally started with an "TS" or "ts"
it will be stripped out and placed in a separate location, aids in matching, usable to genre mark content.

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.name
documentation:
The remained of a file, usually between the date and video markers such as XXX, 4k, etc.   Heavy lifting
occurs to match this to a scene name, perform names, or a combo of both.

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.extension
documentation: The file's extension .mp4 or .mkv

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.source_file_name
documentation: What was originally parsed.

class_variables.name: namer.fileinfo.FileInfo.hashes
documentation: File hashes.

Represents info parsed from a file name, usually of a nzb, named something like:
'EvilAngel.22.01.03.Carmela.Clutch.Fabulous.Anal.3-Way.XXX.2160p.MP4-GAYME-xpost'
or
'DorcelClub.20.12..Aya.Benetti.Megane.Lopez.And.Bella.Tina.2160p.MP4-GAYME-xpost'

modules.name: namer.moviexml
documentation:
Reads movie.xml (your.movie.name.nfo) of Emby/Jellyfin format in to a LookedUpFileInfo,
allowing the metadata to be written in to video files (currently only mp4's),
or used in renaming the video file.

functions.name: namer.moviexml.parse_movie_xml_file
documentation: Parse an Emby/Jellyfin xml file and creates a LookedUpFileInfo from the data.

functions.name: namer.moviexml.write_movie_xml_file
documentation: Parse porndb info and create an Emby/Jellyfin xml file from the data.

functions.name: namer.moviexml.write_nfo
documentation: Writes an .nfo to the correct place for a video file.

modules.name: namer.web.routes.web
documentation: Defines the web routes of a Flask webserver for namer.

functions.name: namer.web.routes.web.get_routes
documentation: Builds a blueprint for flask with passed in context, the NamerConfig.

modules.name: namer.namer
documentation:
This file can process individual movie files in place.
There name, or directory name, will be analyzed, matched against
the porndb, and used for renaming (in place), and updating a mp4
file's metadata (poster, artists, etc.)

functions.name: namer.namer.dir_with_sub_dirs_to_process
documentation:
Used to find sub-dirs of a directory to be individually processed.
The directories will be scanned for media and named/tagged in place
based on config settings.

functions.name: namer.namer.tag_in_place
documentation:
Uses ComparisonResults to update a mp4 file's metadata based on a match in
ComparisonResults.   Expects the first item of list to be the match if there is one.
Will download a poster as well depending on NamerConfig config setting.

functions.name: namer.namer.get_local_metadata_if_requested
documentation:
If there is an .nfo file next to the video_file, attempt to read it as
an Emby/Jellyfin style movie xml file.

functions.name: namer.namer.process_file
documentation:
Bread and butter method.
Given a file, determines if it's a dir, if so, the dir name may be used
for comparison with the porndb.   The larges mp4/mkv file in the directory
or any subdirectories will be assumed to be the movie file.

Does not properly handle multipart movies.

If the input file is not a dir it's name will be used, and it is assumed to
be the movie file we wish to tag.

The movie is either renamed in place if a file, or renamed and move to the root
of the dir if a dir was passed in.

The file is then update based on the metadata from the porndb if a mp4.

functions.name: namer.namer.add_extra_artifacts
documentation: Once the file is in its final location we will grab other relevant output if requested.

functions.name: namer.namer.check_arguments
documentation: check arguments.

functions.name: namer.namer.main
documentation:
Used to tag and rename files from the command line.
See usage function above.

classes.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.search_results
documentation: True if a match was found in the porndb.

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.new_metadata
documentation:
New metadata found for the file being processed.
Sourced including queries against the porndb, which would be stored in search_results,
or reading a .nfo xml file next to the video, with the file name identical except for
the extension, which would be .nfo instead of .mp4,.mkv,.avi,.mov,.flv.

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.dir_file
documentation:
Set if the input file for naming was a directory.   This has advantages, as clean up of other files is now possible,
or all files can be moved to a destination specified in the field final_name_relative.

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.video_file
documentation: The location of the found video file.

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.parsed_file
documentation: The parsed file name.

class_variables.name: namer.namer.ProcessingResults.final_name_relative
documentation: This is the full NamerConfig.new_relative_path_name string with all substitutions made.

Returned from the namer.py process() function.   It contains information about if a match
was found, and of so, where files were placed.  It also tracks if a directory was inputted
to namer (rather than the exact movie file.)  That knowledge can be used to move directories
and preserve relative files, or to delete left over artifacts.

A wrapper allowing shutdown of a Flask server.

Handle matching data in a FileInfo (likely generated by a namer_file_parser.py) to

Give parsed file name parts, and a porndb token, returns a sorted list of possible matches.
Matches will appear first.

functions.name: namer.metadataapi.main
documentation: Looks up metadata from theporndb.net base on file name.

documentation: Currently unused, useful to inspect Flask rules.

functions.name: namer.web.actions.get_phash_results

documentation: Checks if a file belong to namer.

modules.name: namer.ffmpeg
documentation:
ffmpeg is access through this file, it is used to find the video stream's resolution,
and update audio streams "Default" setting.   Apple video players require there be
only one default audio stream, and this script lets you set it with the correct language
code if there are more than one audio streams and if they are correctly labeled.
See:  https://iso639-3.sil.org/code_tables/639/data/ for language codes.

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.index
documentation: stream number

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.codec_name
documentation: "mp3", "h264", "hvec", "png"

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.codec_type
documentation: "audio" or "video"

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.disposition_default
documentation: default stream of this type

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.disposition_attached_pic
documentation: is the "video" stream an attached picture.

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.duration
documentation: seconds

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.bit_rate
documentation: bitrate of the track

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.tags_language
documentation: 3 letters representing language of track (only matters for audio)

class_variables.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFProbeStream.avg_frame_rate
documentation: average frames per second

methods.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFMpeg.ffprobe
documentation: Get the typed results of probing a video stream with ffprobe.

methods.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFMpeg._ffprobe
documentation: Get the typed results of probing a video stream with ffprobe.

methods.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFMpeg.get_audio_stream_for_lang
documentation:
given a mp4 input file and a desired language will return the stream position of that language in the mp4.
if the language is None, or the stream is not found, or the desired stream is the only default stream, None is returned.
See: https://iso639-3.sil.org/code_tables/639/data/

Returns -1 if stream can not be determined

methods.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFMpeg.update_audio_stream_if_needed
documentation:
Returns true if the file had to be edited to have a default audio stream equal to the desired language,
mostly a concern for apple players (Quicktime/Apple TV/etc.)
Copies, and potentially updates the default audio stream of a video file.

methods.name: namer.ffmpeg.FFMpeg.convert
documentation: Attempt to fix corrupt mp4 files.

modules.name: namer.__main__
documentation:
Namer, the porn db file renamer. It can be a command line tool to rename mp4/mkv/avi/mov/flv files and to embed tags in mp4's,
or a watchdog service to do the above watching a directory for new files.  File names are assumed to be of
the form SITE.[YY]YY.MM.DD.String.of.performers.and.or.scene.name.<IGNORED_INFO>.[mp4|mkv|...].   In the name, read the
periods, ".", as any number of spaces " ", dashes "-", or periods ".".

Provided you have an access token to the porndb (free sign up) https://www.theporndb.net/, this program will
attempt to match your file's name to search results from the porndb.   Please note that the site must at least be
a substring of the actual site name on the porndb, and the date must be within one day or the release date on the
porndb for a match to be considered.  If the log file flag is enabled then a <original file name minus ext>_namer.json.gz
file will be written with all the potential matches sorted, descending by how closely the scene name/performer names
match the file.

functions.name: namer.__main__.create_default_config_if_missing
documentation: Find or create config.

functions.name: namer.__main__.main
documentation: Call main method in namer.namer or namer.watchdog.

modules.name: namer.command
documentation: Tools for working with files and directories in namer.

functions.name: namer.command.write_log_file
documentation:
Given porndb scene results sorted by how closely they match a file,  write the contents
of the result matches to a log file with json pickle, the ui could reconstitute the results

functions.name: namer.command.set_permissions
documentation:
Given a file or dir, set permissions from NamerConfig.set_file_permissions,
NamerConfig.set_dir_permissions, and uid/gid if set for the current process recursively.

functions.name: namer.command.move_to_final_location
documentation:
Moves a file or directory to its final location after verifying there is no collision.
Should a collision occur, the file is appropriately renamed to avoid collision.

functions.name: namer.command.gather_target_files_from_dir
documentation: Find files to process in a target directory.

functions.name: namer.command.__exact_command
documentation:
Given a target movie file and a target containing directory, parse appropriate names as determined by
config, aka, "prefer_dir_name_if_available".

functions.name: namer.command.find_target_file
documentation: returns largest matching file

functions.name: namer.command.make_command
documentation: after finding target directory and target movie from input, returns file name descriptors.

functions.name: namer.command.make_command_relative_to
documentation:
Ensure we are going to handle the directory relative to another directory, rather than just the file
specified

functions.name: namer.command.main
documentation: Attempt to parse a name.

classes.name: namer.command.Command

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.input_file
documentation:
This is the original user/machine input of a target path.
If this path is a directory a movie is found within it (recursively).
If this file is a the movie file itself, the parent directory is calculated.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.target_movie_file
documentation: The movie file this name is targeting.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.target_directory
documentation:
The containing directory of a File.  This may be the immediate parent directory, or higher up, depending
on whether a directory was selected as the input to a naming process.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.parsed_dir_name
documentation: Was the input file a directory and is parsing directory names configured?

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.parsed_file
documentation: The parsed file name.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.inplace
documentation: Was the command told to keep the files in place.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.write_from_nfos
documentation: Should .nfo files be used as a source of metadata and writen into file tag info and used for naming.

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.tpdb_id
documentation: The _id used to identify video in tpdb

class_variables.name: namer.command.Command.is_auto
documentation: If False then it means command was from web ui
noinspection PyDataclass

modules.name: namer.mutagen
documentation: Updates mp4 files with metadata tags readable by Plex and Apple TV App.

functions.name: namer.mutagen.resolution_to_hdv_setting
documentation: Using the resolution (height) of a video stream return the atom value for hd video

functions.name: namer.mutagen.set_single_if_not_none

functions.name: namer.mutagen.set_array_if_not_none
documentation: Set an array atom on the video if it is not None.

functions.name: namer.mutagen.update_mp4_file
documentation:
us-tv|TV-MA|600|
us-tv|TV-14|500|
us-tv|TV-PG|400|
us-tv|TV-G|300|
us-tv|TV-Y|200|
us-tv|TV-Y7|100|

mpaa|UNRATED|600|

functions.name: namer.videophash.imagehash._binary_array_to_hex
documentation: internal function to make a hex string out of a binary array.

functions.name: namer.videophash.imagehash.hex_to_hash
documentation:
Convert a stored hash (hex, as retrieved from str(Imagehash))
back to an Imagehash object.

Notes:
1. This algorithm assumes all hashes are either
        bidimensional arrays with dimensions hash_size * hash_size,
        or one-dimensional arrays with dimensions binbits * 14.
2. This algorithm does not work for hash_size < 2.

variables.name: namer.videophash.imagehash.NDArray
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: namer.videophash.imagehash.ImageHash
documentation: Hash encapsulation. Can be used for dictionary keys and comparisons.

classes.name: namer.name_formatter.PartialFormatter
documentation: Used for formatting NamerConfig.inplace_name and NamerConfig.

modules.name: namer.watchdog
documentation:
A file watching service to rename movie files and move them
to relevant locations after match the file against the porndb.

functions.name: namer.watchdog.done_copying
documentation:
Determines if a file is being copied by checking its size in 2 second
increments and seeing if the size has stayed the same.

functions.name: namer.watchdog.handle
documentation: Responsible for processing and moving new movie files.

functions.name: namer.watchdog.retry_failed
documentation: Moves the contents from the failed dir to the watch dir to attempt reprocessing.

functions.name: namer.watchdog.is_fs_case_sensitive
documentation: Create a temporary file to determine if a filesystem is case-sensitive, or not.

functions.name: namer.watchdog.create_watcher
documentation: Configure and start a watchdog looking for new Movies.

classes.name: namer.watchdog.MovieEventHandler
documentation:
When a new movie file is detected, this class handles the event,
and passes off the processing to the handler() function above.

classes.name: namer.watchdog.MovieWatcher

methods.name: namer.watchdog.MovieWatcher.run
documentation:
Checks for new files in 3 second intervals,
needed if running in docker as events aren't properly passed in.

methods.name: namer.watchdog.MovieWatcher.start
documentation: starts a background thread to check for files.

methods.name: namer.watchdog.MovieWatcher.stop
documentation:
stops a background thread to check for files.
Waits for the event processor to complete.

Watches a configured dir for new files and attempts
to process them and move to a destination dir.

If a failure occurs moves the new files to a failed dir.

See NamerConfig

classes.name: namer.comparison_results.Performer

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.Performer.image
documentation:
if available the performers gender, stored as a role.  example: "Female", "Male"
Useful as many nzbs often don't include the scene name, but instead female performers names,
or sometimes both.
Other performers are also used in name matching, if females are attempted first.
Minimal info about a performer, name, and role.

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.LookedUpFileInfo.is_collected

classes.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult

methods.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.is_phash_match
documentation: Returns true if match is a phash match.

methods.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.is_match
documentation:
Returns true if site and creation data match exactly, and if the name fuzzes against
the metadate to 90% or more (via RapidFuzz, and various concatenations of metadata about
actors and scene name) or is a phash match.

methods.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.is_super_match
documentation:
Returns true if site and creation data match exactly, and if the name fuzzes against
the metadate to 95% or more (via RapidFuzz, and various concatenations of metadata about
actors and scene name) and is a phash match.

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.name_match
documentation:
How closely did the name found in FileInfo match (via RapidFuzz string comparison)
The performers and scene name found in LookedUpFileInfo.  Various combinations of performers
and scene namer are used for attempted matching.

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.site_match
documentation: Did the studios match between filenameparts and looked up

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.date_match
documentation: Did the dates match between filenameparts and looked up

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.name_parts
documentation: Parts of the file name that were parsed and used as search parameters.

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.looked_up
documentation:
Info pulled from the porndb.  When doing searches it will not include tags, only included when
performing a lookup by id (which is done only after a match is made.)

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.phash_distance
documentation: How close searched hash to database one.

class_variables.name: namer.comparison_results.ComparisonResult.phash_duration
documentation: Duration diff with phash duration.

Represents the comparison from a FileInfo and a LookedUpFileInfo, it will be
considered a match if the creation dates match, the studio matches, and the original
scene/perform part of a file name can match any combination of the metadata about
actor names and/or scene name.   RapidFuzz is used to make the comparison.
